words.def: a word or phrase that is easy to remember, used for example by a political party or in advertising to attract people’s attention or to suggest an idea quickly
words.example: an advertising slogan

words.def: a surface or piece of land that slopes (= is higher at one end than the other)
words.example: on a slope, The town is built on a slope.

words.def: used to show that you do not think that the word or phrase that is being used to describe somebody/something is appropriate
words.example: the opinion of a so-called ‘expert’

words.def: the top layer of the earth in which plants, trees, etc. grow
words.example: instruments for measuring soil moisture

words.def: of or connected with the sun
words.example: solar radiation

words.def: in a way that is not known or certain
words.example: We must stop him from seeing her somehow.

words.def: at a time that you do not know exactly or has not yet been decided
words.example: I saw him sometime last summer.

words.def: to some degree
words.example: somewhat different/similar

words.def: clever and complicated in the way that it works or is presented
words.example: highly sophisticated computer systems

words.def: the spiritual part of a person, believed to exist after death
words.example: He believed his immortal soul was in peril.

words.def: having or involving expert knowledge of a particular area of work, study or medicine
words.example: specialist knowledge/training/skills

words.def: to become an expert in a particular area of work, study or business; to spend more time on one area of work, etc. than on others
words.example: Many students prefer not to specialize too soon.

words.def: a group into which animals, plants, etc. that are able to have sex with each other and produce healthy young are divided, smaller than a genus and identified by a Latin name
words.example: a conservation area for endangered species

words.def: connected with or intended for one particular thing only
words.example: liquid vitamins specifically designed for children

words.def: to state something, especially by giving an exact measurement, time, exact instructions, etc.
words.example: specify something, Remember to specify your size when ordering clothes.

words.def: very impressive
words.example: The coastal road has spectacular scenery.

words.def: a person who is watching an event, especially a sports event
words.example: The new football stadium will hold 75 000 spectators.

words.def: to form an opinion about something without knowing all the details or facts
words.example: speculate (about/on/as to something), We all speculated about the reasons for her resignation.

words.def: the act of forming opinions about what has happened or what might happen without knowing all the facts
words.example: His private life is the subject of much speculation.

words.def: the rate at which somebody/something moves or travels
words.example: at … speed, at high/low/full/top speed

words.def: one of the various types of powder or seed that come from plants and are used in cooking. Spices have a strong taste and smell.
words.example: common spices such as ginger and cinnamon

words.def: to flow over the edge of a container by accident; to make liquid do this
words.example: Water had spilled out of the bucket onto the floor.

words.def: connected with the human spirit, rather than the body or physical things
words.example: a spiritual experience

words.def: a feeling of wanting to hurt or upset somebody
words.example: out of spite, I'm sure he only said it out of spite.

words.def: an argument or disagreement that divides a group of people or makes somebody separate from somebody else
words.example: Ten years after their acrimonious split, the band has reformed.

words.def: to change something good into something bad, unpleasant, etc.
words.example: Our camping trip was spoilt by bad weather.

words.def: a person who speaks for a group or an organization
words.example: a police spokesman

words.def: a person who speaks for a group or an organization
words.example: a council spokesperson

words.def: a woman who speaks for a group or an organization
words.example: a government spokeswoman

words.def: a person or company that pays for a radio or television programme, or for a concert or sporting event, usually in return for advertising
words.example: The programme is funded by a number of corporate sponsors.

words.def: financial support from a sponsor
words.example: a $50 million sponsorship deal

words.def: connected with sports
words.example: a major sporting event

words.def: a small round area that has a different colour or feels different from the surface it is on
words.example: Which has spots, the leopard or the tiger?

words.def: an increase in the amount or number of something that there is, or in the area that is affected by something
words.example: measures to halt the spread of the disease

words.def: the season between winter and summer when plants begin to grow
words.example: The following spring, the three artists travelled to California.

words.def: fixed or steady; not likely to move, change or fail
words.example: The patient's condition is stable (= it is not getting worse).

words.def: a period or state that something/somebody passes through while developing or making progress
words.example: at… stage, I can't make a decision at this stage.

words.def: a table or small shop with an open front that people sell things from, especially at a market
words.example: He works on a market stall in the Square.

words.def: the opinions that somebody has about something and expresses publicly
words.example: What is the newspaper's stance on the war?

words.def: an attitude towards something or an opinion that you make clear to people
words.example: He has avoided taking a firm stand.

words.def: average or normal rather than having special or unusual features
words.example: the standard rate of tax (= paid by everyone)

words.def: to look at somebody/something for a long time
words.example: I screamed and everyone stared.

words.def: to suffer or die because you do not have enough food to eat; to make somebody suffer or die in this way
words.example: The animals were left to starve to death.

words.def: provided or controlled by the government of a country
words.example: state officials/agencies

words.def: the legal position of a person, group or country
words.example: They were granted refugee status.

words.def: gradually and in an even and regular way
words.example: The company's exports have been increasing steadily.

words.def: developing, growing, etc. gradually and in an even and regular way
words.example: We've had five years of steady economic growth.

words.def: the hot gas that water changes into when it boils
words.example: Steam rose from the boiling kettle.

words.def: a strong, hard metal that is made of a mixture of iron and carbon
words.example: the iron and steel industry

words.def: rising or falling quickly, not gradually
words.example: a steep hill/slope

words.def: one of a series of things that you do in order to achieve something
words.example: We are taking steps to prevent pollution.

words.def: a thin piece of wood that has fallen or been broken from a tree
words.example: We collected dry sticks to start a fire.

words.def: made of or covered in a substance that sticks to things that touch it
words.example: sticky fingers covered in jam

words.def: difficult to bend or move
words.example: a sheet of stiff black cardboard

words.def: to make something develop or become more active; to encourage something
words.example: The exhibition has stimulated interest in her work.

words.def: a supply of goods that is available for sale in a shop
words.example: We have a fast turnover of stock.
